key_insight: A unified, energy-based CutFEM formulation for finite-strain elasticity lets residuals and tangents come directly from one augmented functional, enabling model-independent hyperelasticity with automatic differentiation.
engineering_relevance: This provides a robust unfitted finite-element route for complex solid mechanics geometries, with cut-independent stability and optimal convergence for regular problems.
method: Build the discrete problem from an augmented energy: bulk hyperelastic energy + Nitsche boundary terms + ghost-penalty stabilization.
Use automatic differentiation to derive the first Piola–Kirchhoff stress and elasticity tensor directly from the scalar energy density.
Analyze each Newton linearization to prove cut-independent coercivity, continuity, and an O(h^-2) condition-number bound.
evidence_signal: The method shows optimal h-convergence for linear, quadratic, and cubic elements on a smooth benchmark, and the corner-singularity analysis explains the observed accuracy limit at mixed boundary junctions.
watch_item: Accuracy can still be capped by corner singularities at mixed Dirichlet–Neumann junctions, so local mesh refinement may be needed to recover optimal rates.
paper_title: A Unified CutFEM Formulation for Finite-Strain Elasticity: Energy Minimisation and Corner Singularities
